title: Basgo Monastery
tagline: Basgo Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Basgo Monastery, also known as Basgo or Bazgo Gompa, is a Buddhist monastery located in Basgo or Bazgo on the bank of Indus River in Leh District of Ladakh in northern India approx
overview.description: Basgo Monastery, also known as Basgo or Bazgo Gompa, is a Buddhist monastery located in Basgo or Bazgo on the bank of Indus River in Leh District of Ladakh in northern India approximately 5 km west of Nimoo and 40 km east from Leh. Although the monastery was built for the Namgyal rulers in 1680, Bazgo itself was embedded in the early days of Ladakh and is frequently mentioned in the Ladakhi Chronicles when it was a political and cultural center. In the 15th century, a palace was built in Basgo. The monastery is situated on top of the hill towering over the ruins of the ancient town and is note

topPlaces.name: Basgo
topPlaces.description: Basgo or Bazgoo, is a village situated on the bank of the Indus River in Leh district, Ladakh, India. It was once an important cultural and political centre and is frequently mentioned in the Ladakhi Chronicles.

topPlaces.name: Nimo, Ladakh
topPlaces.description: Nimo or Nyemo (Tibetan: སྙེ་མོ་, Wylie: snye mo) is a village and the headquarters of an eponymous community development block in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil, 35 km from Leh.

topPlaces.name: Ney, Leh
topPlaces.description: Ney is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Magnetic Hill (India)
topPlaces.description: Magnet Hill is a gravity hill located, between Khalatse and Leh in Sham district of Ladakh, India. The layout of the area and surrounding slopes create the optical illusion of a hill.

topPlaces.name: Likir Monastery
topPlaces.description: Likir Monastery or Likir Gompa (Klud-kyil) is a Buddhist monastery in Ladakh, Northern India. It is located at 3700m elevation, approximately 52 kilometres (32 mi) west of Leh.

topPlaces.name: Nimoo Bazgo Hydroelectric Plant
topPlaces.description: The Nimoo Bazgo Power Project is a run-of-the-river power project on the Indus River situated at Alchi village, 75 kilometres (47 mi) from Leh in the Indian Union Territory of Ladakh. The project was conceived on 1 July 2001 and approved on 8 June 2005, and construction began on 23rd Sept, 2006.

topPlaces.name: Likir
topPlaces.description: Likir is a village and eponymous Subdivision in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil, in the Ladakh region.

items.note: Basgo or Bazgoo, is a village situated on the bank of the Indus River in Leh district, Ladakh, India. It was once an important cultural and political centre and is frequently mentioned in the Ladakhi Chronicles.

Nimo or Nyemo (Tibetan: སྙེ་མོ་, Wylie: snye mo) is a village and the headquarters of an eponymous community development block in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil, 35 km from Leh.
Ney is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.
Magnet Hill is a gravity hill located, between Khalatse and Leh in Sham district of Ladakh, India. The layout of the area and surrounding slopes create the optical illusion of a hill.
Likir Monastery or Likir Gompa (Klud-kyil) is a Buddhist monastery in Ladakh, Northern India. It is located at 3700m elevation, approximately 52 kilometres (32 mi) west of Leh.

The Nimoo Bazgo Power Project is a run-of-the-river power project on the Indus River situated at Alchi village, 75 kilometres (47 mi) from Leh in the Indian Union Territory of Ladakh. The project was conceived on 1 July 2001 and approved on 8 June 2005, and construction began on 23rd Sept, 2006.
Likir is a village and eponymous Subdivision in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil, in the Ladakh region.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Basgo Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Basgo Monastery is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Basgo Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (26 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (256 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Basgo Monastery?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Basgo Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Basgo Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Basgo Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Basgo Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Basgo, Nimo, Ladakh, Ney, Leh, Magnetic Hill (India), Likir Monastery.
